title: lethedon setosa
intro: Lethedon setosa, also known as the Yellow Jessamine or Woodbine, is a perennial woody vine native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its clusters of bright yellow, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Lethedon setosa include relieving anxiety and insomnia, as well as providing antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in some herbal remedies for its sedative and antispasmodic properties.
